title: CGAP Human Lung Dissociation
steps: Cut lung into 1cm x 1cm sections and ensure weight is ~0.2g.
Transfer the piece of tissue to a 10cm petri dish and add ~250µl Digestion Medium to cover it. Digestion Media DMEM + Collagenase D (0.1ml/ml) + DNase I (10µl/ml)
Using two scalpels, chop the piece inside the tube as finely as possible.
Add ~2ml of Digestion Media and transfer the mashed tissue to a 15ml falcon tube using a 5ml stripette.
Wash the dish with 1ml of Digestion Medium, transferring it to the 15ml falcon tube with the tissue.

Transfer it to an incubator at 37°C for 1 hour on a rocker (tissue from lower left lobe should be left for 30 minutes). Freeze the tissue samples at this stage CGAP Freezing Human Tissue in Isopentane by Adam Hunter

Collect the sample and filter the cells through a 100µm nylon mesh filter into a 50ml falcon tube. Using the plunger of a syringe, repeatedly mash the filter and rinse with cold Complete Medium up to 25ml. Complete Media DMEM + 10% FBS
Spin down 360xg for 10 min at 4°C. Acceleration 4, break 2.
Very carefully discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the cell pellet in 25ml of Complete Medium.
Spin down 360xg for 10 min at 4°C. Acceleration 4, break 2.
Very carefully discard the supernatant.
Add 1ml of 1x Red Blood Cell Lysis solution to your cell pellet.
Incubate 5 min on ice with periodic agitation.
Add fresh cold PBS up to 10ml.
Spin down 360xg for 5 min at 4°C.
Discard supernatant.
Resuspend pellet in 1ml of 0.04% BSA and count cells.

If percentage of live cells is higher than 70-80%, cells can then be processed for scRNA-seq. If percentage of live cells is below 70-80%, remove dead cells by following “MACS Live Dead Separation”. CGAP MACS Live Dead Separation by Adam Hunter
Dilute cells to 2x10 6 cell per ml in 0.04% BSA and proceed to 10X Preparation for scRNA sequencing.

Ensure all unused tissue, equipment and tubes that have been in contact with primary tissue are placed into Virkon in sweetie jar for a minimum of 1 hour. After this time aspirate and disposing in relevant sharps or waste routes.
%s %s
creator: Adam Hunter
